name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: Tonight, I join Americans from coast to coast in remembrance of those we have lost to this virus.   400,000 Americans. Parents. Grandparents. Sisters. Brothers. Even children.   We must honor them by committing to do everything in our power to defeat COVID using science.

tweet: 🚨Good news! The #COVIDVaccine is now available to people in Phase 1B-Tier 1 – including everyone 65 and older in Washington.  More information on eligibility and where you can get your vaccine can be found below and at  https://t.co/IhkCx17DXW  https://t.co/DNEQaNIR6E

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: The Reverend Dr. Martin Luther King Jr. said, "The time is always right to do what is right."  His words continue to inspire us to this day. tweet: This is what patriotism looks like.   Thank you, Eugene Goodman, for your bravery and heroism.   https://t.co/ZI7LOXVENh

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: 🚨$600 was a down payment, not enough to weather this crisis.   I support Biden’s #AmericanRescuePlan b/c it is the bold investment #WA10 needs to recover & safely reopen:  ✅$1,400 checks ✅$ for states & cities ✅national vaccination & testing ✅small biz relief  ✅housing aid

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: Congrats to my good friend and colleague @GovInslee on his historic inauguration for a 3rd term! He is the 1st incumbent elected to a 3rd term in #Washingtonstate in more than 40 years. I know his leadership will help us build a brighter future for all Washingtonians.  #WAleg

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: For me, being #KoreanAmerican goes back to the values of my mother, a #Korean immigrant. Her resilience, strength and hard work reflect the rich values that the Korean-American community continues to offer our nation. Happy #KoreanAmericanDay!  https://t.co/9JFoVJb93u

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: I did not come to Congress to #impeach the president...   But I took the Oath of Office 10 days ago, and that means defending our Constitution against all enemies, foreign and domestic.   The House took swift and necessary bipartisan action today to do just that.   Read more⬇️  https://t.co/o4z6xPzdm4

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: Donald Trump is a clear and present threat. If @VP @Mike_Pence won't stand up for our democracy or Constitution, we will.   We did not allow domestic terrorism to derail our Constitutional duty last week.    We won't be deterred now.   #25thAmendment #ImpeachmentNow #ImpeachTrump

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: We have lost over 375,000 Americans to this virus.   Of the Americans we've lost, a disproportionate number are Black. This is in no way acceptable.  As we battle this virus, public health officials have a duty to address the widening disparities we are seeing in our communities.

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: I met w/ the Tacoma-Pierce County Black Collective to reflect on the work that lies ahead to support our community. From the disproportionate harm of COVID to the double standards in policing we saw just last wk, it's clear we can't just recover & rebuild-we must do so equitably.

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: 🚨Have you received your stimulus check? Congress’ recent COVID relief package included another round of $600 in direct payments to most Americans. If you haven’t received your direct deposit from the IRS, your check may have gone out by mail. Learn more ➡️ https://t.co/y1fQYXB2OT

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: I have added my name to the articles of impeachment that @RepCicilline, @RepTedLieu, and @RepRaskin are introducing to remove @realDonaldTrump. This isn’t just about protecting our democracy for the next 12 days, we must ensure that Trump can never do such grave harm ever again.

tweet: Congress must remain in session to address this act of domestic terrorism. Those who have incited violence or contributed to it have gravely undermined our democracy, and they must face the consequences. We call for Congress to reconvene without delay.  https://t.co/GqupYCFWYd

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: .@realDonaldTrump needs to be removed from power immediately. He has shown time and time again that he is not only unfit to serve, but also unstable. He represents the most pressing threat to our national security and democracy, even with only 13 days left in his term.

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: I came to work today to certify a free & fair election. Then Confederate-flag toting domestic terrorists attempted a coup, incited by Trump. Make no mistake: We will not allow this violence to silence the people's voice. We will validate the over 81M Americans who elected Biden.

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: The Capitol has been breached. Both chambers have been evacuated. This is not peaceful protest, this is domestic terrorism. This violence is direct result of Trump undermining our democratic process simply because he didn't like the results. Shameful and completely reckless.

tweet: "As a woman of both Korean-American & African-American descent, it was deeply personal to wear my #hanbok, which not only symbolizes my heritage & honors my mother, but also serves as a larger testament to the importance of #diversity in our nation, state, & the People's House."

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: Trump's @EPA finalized a harmful new rule today that undermines science & the agency’s own mission to protect human health & the environment. The effects of toxic pollution are devastating. We cannot let this anti-science rule stand. #CensoredScience #ScienceNotSilence

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: Thank you @RepDerekKilmer! Looking forward to working together to deliver for Washingtonians and #WA10 in the #117thCongress. #ForThePeople

name: Congresswoman Marilyn Strickland
tweet: Congress must look like the people it represents. Today, we passed an inclusive and comprehensive 117th rules package – ensuring all Americans can see themselves in their Congress. It also embraces bipartisanship, technology, ethics and accountability standards.
